analysis: Markets edged higher across the board today, with the **S&P 500** gaining **+0.26%** to **7,041**, the **Dow** adding **+0.24%** to **48,579**, and the **Nasdaq** leading at **+0.36%** to **24,103**, while the small-cap **Russell 2000** kept pace with a **+0.22%** advance. The **VIX** pulled back **-1.27%** to **17.94**, signaling a modest easing of near-term anxiety as risk appetite quietly improved. Energy (**XLE +1.47%**), Communication Services (**XLC +1.25%**), and Technology (**XLK +1.14%**) led sector gains, powered by a strong surge in chip and cloud names — **AMD** surged **+7.80%** to **$278.26**, **INTC** jumped **+5.48%**, and **ORCL** climbed **+5.02%** — while **Health Care** (**XLV -0.79%**), **Industrials** (**XLI -0.50%**), and **Consumer Discretionary** (**XLY -0.47%**) lagged, with **AAPL** slipping **-1.14%** to **$263.40** as a notable drag on the mega-cap space.
